steps.title: Find the role the underlined sentence plays
steps.content: The underlined sentence opens with However, signaling a contrast, and introduces the Trademark Act provision allowing trademarks to be taken away. Everything that follows — the cellophane example, the “genericide” process — develops this exact point. The sentence launches a new direction the passage hadn’t taken before.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It concludes the text’s argument about how the Trademark Act of 1946 can be improved.’: The passage never argues for improvements to the Act — it explains how it works. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It completes the text’s comparison of trademark laws in the US to laws in other countries.’: No other countries are mentioned anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It adds to the text’s description of products made by Kimberly-Clark.’: Kimberly-Clark is a later example, not the focus of this sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It begins the text’s discussion of the fact that trademarks can be taken away.’: This is exactly what the sentence does — it introduces the possibility of losing a trademark, which the rest of the passage elaborates. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
